incidents.victim: Government agencies, military, critical infrastructure worldwide (200+ organizations)
incidents.vector: Exploited known vulnerabilities in 18,000+ end-of-life SOHO routers (TP-Link, Mikrotik) to hijack DNS settings and intercept OAuth authentication tokens via adversary-in-the-middle attacks on Microsoft 365.
incidents.impact: Harvested credentials, emails, and authentication tokens from 200+ organizations and 5,000 consumer devices across 120+ countries without deploying malware. FBI conducted court-authorized 'Operation Masquerade' to remediate compromised U.S. routers.

incidents.vector: Password-spray attacks and exploitation of unpatched internet-facing systems targeting U.S. critical infrastructure, escalating since the start of the Iran conflict.
incidents.impact: CISA and FBI warned of escalating disruptive attacks against energy and water facilities. Multiple utilities reported intrusion attempts; full scope of compromises still under assessment.

incidents.victim: Axios open-source project users (est. 600,000 downloads affected)
incidents.vector: Social-engineered the lead Axios developer via a fake company and fraudulent video call to install malware, then hijacked the npm account to publish poisoned Axios versions containing remote access trojans.
incidents.impact: Malicious versions were available for several hours before removal, potentially compromising ~600,000 downstream installations. Malware targeted macOS, Windows, and Linux, stealing credentials, API keys, and SSH keys.

incidents.vector: Gained access to the Commission's AWS account via the Trivy supply chain compromise (attributed to TeamPCP), then used a stolen Amazon API key to exfiltrate data from the Europa.eu cloud infrastructure.
incidents.impact: 92 GB of compressed data stolen, including 52,000 files related to outbound email communications. Data from 42 internal clients and at least 29 EU entities may have been compromised. Stolen data later appeared on ShinyHunters dark web site.

incidents.victim: Aqua Security Trivy users, LiteLLM users, and downstream organizations
incidents.vector: TeamPCP compromised the Trivy vulnerability scanner via GitHub Actions supply chain attack, injecting credential-stealing malware into official releases. Also compromised LiteLLM and other open-source projects.
incidents.impact: Malicious Trivy versions harvested SSH keys, cloud credentials, Kubernetes tokens, and cryptocurrency wallets. Cascading compromises affected multiple downstream organizations including the European Commission.

incidents.victim: Iranian infrastructure operators (Kubernetes clusters, local machines)
incidents.vector: TeamPCP deployed a wiper payload via the same infrastructure used in the Trivy attack; malware checks for Iran's timezone and Farsi locale before executing.
incidents.impact: If the target matches Iran-related criteria and has Kubernetes cluster access, the wiper destroys data on every node; otherwise it wipes the local machine. Full scope of destruction unknown.

incidents.vector: Phishing attack targeting a senior attorney; Silent Ransom Group gained access to a limited number of dated client files.
incidents.impact: Files for 10 clients were accessed and posted to the group's leak site. Jones Day notified all affected clients. The firm has previously been targeted in the 2021 Clop file-transfer hack.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized network access detected on March 28; attack vector not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Hasbro took systems offline, activated business continuity plan, and warned of potential order fulfillment delays lasting several weeks. Share price fell 3%. Investigation ongoing with third-party cybersecurity professionals.

incidents.vector: Medusa ransomware operators exploited web-facing vulnerabilities, moving from initial access to data exfiltration and encryption within 24 hours.
incidents.impact: Hospital operations severely disrupted; UMMC fully reopened on March 2 with FBI and DHS assistance. Medusa claimed responsibility and threatened to publish stolen data.

incidents.vector: Medusa ransomware group exploited perimeter vulnerabilities to gain initial access; used legitimate remote management tools for lateral movement.
incidents.impact: County government systems disrupted. Medusa claimed the attack and listed Passaic County on its leak site.

incidents.vector: Exploited a vulnerability (CVE-2026-3502) in TrueConf video conferencing software's update mechanism to distribute weaponized updates to dozens of government agencies from a compromised on-premises server.
incidents.impact: Dozens of government entities across the target country received malicious updates. Attackers deployed the Havoc penetration testing tool and ShadowPad malware for persistent access. CISA ordered U.S. agencies to patch by April 16.

incidents.victim: Organizations running Next.js applications (hundreds of hosts)
incidents.vector: Large-scale automated exploitation of React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) in Next.js applications using a custom framework called 'NEXUS Listener' to extract credentials from compromised hosts.
incidents.impact: Cloud tokens, database credentials, and SSH keys harvested from hundreds of compromised hosts. Centralized aggregation of stolen data enables follow-on attacks and lateral movement.

incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2026-35616 (severity 9.1/10), an improper access control vulnerability in FortiClient EMS allowing unauthenticated remote code execution.
incidents.impact: CISA added to KEV catalog and gave federal agencies until Thursday to apply hotfix. Second FortiClient EMS vulnerability in three weeks; exploitation ramped up over Easter weekend when security teams were at reduced capacity.

incidents.victim: Organizations using F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager
incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2025-53521, an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in F5 BIG-IP APM, reclassified from DoS to critical RCE.
incidents.impact: F5 confirmed active exploitation. NCSC UK urged immediate investigation for compromise on all affected products. Widely deployed in large enterprises for access policy management.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-3055 (severity 9.3/10), an out-of-bounds read in Citrix NetScaler allowing unauthenticated attackers to leak sensitive memory — dubbed 'Citrix Bleed 3.'
incidents.impact: CISA ordered federal agencies to patch immediately. Bug has hallmarks of previous Citrix Bleed vulnerabilities used by ransomware gangs and nation-state hackers against hospitals and critical infrastructure.

incidents.victim: Individuals and organizations targeted via messaging apps (WhatsApp, Signal, Messenger)
incidents.vector: Russia-based actors targeting high-risk individuals through messaging apps using phishing, malicious QR codes, and social engineering to hijack accounts or add unauthorized devices.
incidents.impact: NCSC UK and international partners issued joint warning. Attackers attempt to trick targets into sharing login codes, join group chats undetected, and impersonate known contacts to access sensitive information.

incidents.vector: German BKA identified Daniil Shchukin (31, Krasnodar, Russia) as 'UNKN,' the leader of both GandCrab and REvil ransomware operations, along with co-conspirator Anatoly Kravchuk.
incidents.impact: Shchukin and Kravchuk linked to at least 130 acts of computer sabotage, €2M in direct extortion, and €35M in total economic damage across Germany. GandCrab/REvil collectively extorted over $2B globally. Shchukin is believed to be in Russia.

incidents.victim: Operators of Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad botnets (3M+ compromised IoT devices)
incidents.vector: DOJ, FBI, and international partners seized domains and infrastructure behind four botnets that compromised 3M+ IoT devices (routers, webcams) to launch record-breaking DDoS attacks.
incidents.impact: Hundreds of thousands of DDoS attacks disrupted targets including U.S. Department of Defense networks. Infrastructure dismantled and domains seized.

incidents.vector: Jonathan Spalletta exploited smart contract bugs in two separate hacks of the Uranium Finance exchange in April 2021, extracting ~$54.7M in cryptocurrency.
incidents.impact: Uranium Finance shut down due to lack of funds. Spalletta laundered proceeds through Tornado Cash mixer and purchased rare collectibles. DOJ seized ~$31M in cryptocurrency; Spalletta surrendered and faces up to 30 years in prison.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-5281, a use-after-free vulnerability in Dawn (WebGPU implementation) — the fourth Chrome zero-day exploited in attacks in 2026.
incidents.impact: Google issued emergency patch. CISA added to KEV catalog. Exploitation details withheld pending patch adoption.
